children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: The amount of any deduction or credit allowed by this subtitle shall be taken for the taxable year which is the proper taxable year under the method of accounting used in computing taxable income.

children.heading: Special rule in case of death
children.label: b Special rule in case of death
children.text: In the case of the death of a taxpayer whose taxable income is computed under an accrual method of accounting, any amount accrued as a deduction or credit only by reason of the death of the taxpayer shall not be allowed in computing taxable income for the period in which falls the date of the taxpayer’s death.

children.heading: Accrual of real property taxes
children.label: c Accrual of real property taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the taxable income is computed under an accrual method of accounting, then, at the election of the taxpayer, any real property tax which is related to a definite period of time shall be accrued ratably over that period.

children.heading: Without consent
children.label: A Without consent
children.text: A taxpayer may, without the consent of the Secretary, make an election under this subsection for his first taxable year in which he incurs real property taxes. Such an election shall be made not later than the time prescribed by law for filing the return for such year (including extensions thereof).

children.heading: With consent
children.label: B With consent
children.text: A taxpayer may, with the consent of the Secretary, make an election under this subsection at any time.

children.heading: Limitation on acceleration of accrual of taxes
children.label: d Limitation on acceleration of accrual of taxes

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer whose taxable income is computed under an accrual method of accounting, to the extent that the time for accruing taxes is earlier than it would be but for any action of any taxing jurisdiction taken after , then, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, such taxes shall be treated as accruing at the time they would have accrued but for such action by such taxing jurisdiction.December 31, 1960

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, paragraph (1) shall be inapplicable to any item of tax to the extent that its application would (but for this paragraph) prevent all persons (including successors in interest) from ever taking such item into account.

children.heading: Dividends or interest paid on certain deposits or withdrawable accounts
children.label: e Dividends or interest paid on certain deposits or withdrawable accounts
children.text: Except as provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, amounts paid to, or credited to the accounts of, depositors or holders of accounts as dividends or interest on their deposits or withdrawable accounts (if such amounts paid or credited are withdrawable on demand subject only to customary notice to withdraw) by a mutual savings bank not having capital stock represented by shares, a domestic building and loan association, or a cooperative bank shall not be allowed as a deduction for the taxable year to the extent such amounts are paid or credited for periods representing more than 12 months. Any such amount not allowed as a deduction as the result of the application of the preceding sentence shall be allowed as a deduction for such other taxable year as the Secretary determines to be consistent with the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to points paid in respect of any indebtedness incurred in connection with the purchase or improvement of, and secured by, the principal residence of the taxpayer to the extent that, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, such payment of points is an established business practice in the area in which such indebtedness is incurred, and the amount of such payment does not exceed the amount generally charged in such area.

children.heading: Certain liabilities not incurred before economic performance
children.label: h Certain liabilities not incurred before economic performance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this title, in determining whether an amount has been incurred with respect to any item during any taxable year, the all events test shall not be treated as met any earlier than when economic performance with respect to such item occurs.

children.heading: Services and property provided to the tax­payer
children.label: A Services and property provided to the tax­payer
i
children.text: the providing of services to the taxpayer by another person, economic performance occurs as such person provides such services,

children.label: ii
children.text: the providing of property to the taxpayer by another person, economic performance occurs as the person provides such property, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the use of property by the taxpayer, economic performance occurs as the taxpayer uses such property.

children.heading: Services and property provided by the taxpayer
children.label: B Services and property provided by the taxpayer
children.text: If the liability of the taxpayer requires the taxpayer to provide property or services, economic performance occurs as the taxpayer provides such property or services.

children.heading: Workers compensation and tort liabilities of the taxpayer
children.label: C Workers compensation and tort liabilities of the taxpayer
i
children.text: arises under any workers compensation act, or

children.label: ii
children.text: arises out of any tort,

children.heading: Other items
children.label: D Other items
children.text: In the case of any other liability of the taxpayer, economic performance occurs at the time determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: the all events test with respect to such item is met during such taxable year (determined without regard to paragraph (1)),

children.label: I
children.text: a reasonable period after the close of such taxable year, or

children.label: II
children.text: 8½ months after the close of such taxable year,

children.label: iii
children.text: such item is recurring in nature and the taxpayer consistently treats items of such kind as incurred in the taxable year in which the requirements of clause (i) are met, and

children.label: I
children.text: such item is not a material item, or

children.label: II
children.text: the accrual of such item in the taxable year in which the requirements of clause (i) are met results in a more proper match against income than accruing such item in the taxable year in which economic performance occurs.

children.heading: Financial statements considered under subparagraph (A)(iv)
children.label: B Financial statements considered under subparagraph (A)(iv)
children.text: In making a determination under subparagraph (A)(iv), the treatment of such item on financial statements shall be taken into account.

children.heading: Paragraph not to apply to workers compensation and tort liabilities
children.label: C Paragraph not to apply to workers compensation and tort liabilities
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any item described in subparagraph (C) of paragraph (2).

children.heading: All events test
children.label: 4 All events test
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the all events test is met with respect to any item if all events have occurred which determine the fact of liability and the amount of such liability can be determined with reasonable accuracy.

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to certain items
children.label: 5 Subsection not to apply to certain items
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any item for which a deduction is allowable under a provision of this title which specifically provides for a deduction for a reserve for estimated expenses.

children.heading: Special rules for tax shelters
children.label: i Special rules for tax shelters

children.heading: Recurring item exception not to apply
children.label: 1 Recurring item exception not to apply
children.text: In the case of a tax shelter, economic performance shall be determined without regard to paragraph (3) of subsection (h).

children.heading: Special rule for spudding of oil or gas wells
children.label: 2 Special rule for spudding of oil or gas wells

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a tax shelter, economic performance with respect to amounts paid during the taxable year for drilling an oil or gas well shall be treated as having occurred within a taxable year if drilling of the well commences before the close of the 90th day after the close of the taxable year.

children.heading: Deduction limited to cash basis
children.label: B Deduction limited to cash basis

children.heading: Tax shelter partnerships
children.label: i Tax shelter partnerships
children.text: In the case of a tax shelter which is a partnership, in applying section 704(d) to a deduction or loss for any taxable year attributable to an item which is deductible by reason of subparagraph (A), the term “cash basis” shall be substituted for the term “adjusted basis”.

children.heading: Other tax shelters
children.label: ii Other tax shelters
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, in the case of a tax shelter other than a partnership, the aggregate amount of the deductions allowable by reason of subparagraph (A) for any taxable year shall be limited in a manner similar to the limitation under clause (i).

children.label: i

children.label: I
children.text: was arranged by the partnership or by any person who participated in the organization, sale, or management of the partnership (or any person related to such person within the meaning of section 465(b)(3)(C)), or

children.label: II
children.text: was secured by any asset of the partnership.

children.label: A
children.text: any enterprise (other than a C corporation) if at any time interests in such enterprise have been offered for sale in any offering required to be registered with any Federal or State agency having the authority to regulate the offering of securities for sale,

children.label: B
children.text: any syndicate (within the meaning of section 1256(e)(3)(B)), and

children.label: C
children.text: any tax shelter (as defined in section 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)).

children.heading: Special rules for farming
children.label: 4 Special rules for farming
children.text: In the case of the trade or business of farming (as defined in section 464(e)), in determining whether an entity is a tax shelter, the definition of farming syndicate in subsection (k) shall be substituted for subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (3).

children.heading: Economic performance
children.label: 5 Economic performance
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “economic performance” has the meaning given such term by subsection (h).

children.heading: Limitation on excess farm losses of certain taxpayers
children.label: j Limitation on excess farm losses of certain taxpayers

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 1 Limitation
children.text: If a taxpayer other than a C corporation receives any applicable subsidy for any taxable year, any excess farm loss of the taxpayer for the taxable year shall not be allowed.

children.heading: Disallowed loss carried to next taxable year
children.label: 2 Disallowed loss carried to next taxable year
children.text: Any loss which is disallowed under paragraph (1) shall be treated as a deduction of the taxpayer attributable to farming businesses in the next taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: any direct or counter-cyclical payment under title I of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008, or any payment elected to be received in lieu of any such payment, or

children.label: B
children.text: any Commodity Credit Corporation loan.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate deductions of the taxpayer for the taxable year which are attributable to farming businesses of such taxpayer (determined without regard to whether or not such deductions are disallowed for such taxable year under paragraph (1)), over

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate gross income or gain of such taxpayer for the taxable year which is attributable to such farming businesses, plus

children.label: II
children.text: the threshold amount for the taxable year.

children.label: I
children.text: $300,000 ($150,000 in the case of married individuals filing separately), or

children.label: II
children.text: the excess (if any) of the aggregate amounts described in subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) for the 5-consecutive taxable year period preceding the taxable year over the aggregate amounts described in subparagraph (A)(i) for such period.

children.heading: Special rules for determining aggregate amounts
children.label: ii Special rules for determining aggregate amounts
I
children.text: notwithstanding the disregard in subparagraph (A)(i) of any disallowance under paragraph (1), in the case of any loss which is carried forward under paragraph (2) from any taxable year, such loss (or any portion thereof) shall be taken into account for the first taxable year in which a deduction for such loss (or portion) is not disallowed by reason of this subsection, and

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary shall prescribe rules for the computation of the aggregate amounts described in such clause in cases where the filing status of the taxpayer is not the same for the taxable year and each of the taxable years in the period described in such clause.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “farming business” has the meaning given such term in section 263A(e)(4).

children.heading: Certain trades and businesses included
children.label: ii Certain trades and businesses included
I
children.text: the term “farming business” shall include any trade or business of the taxpayer of the processing of such commodity (without regard to whether the processing is incidental to the growing, raising, or harvesting of such commodity), and

children.label: II
children.text: if the taxpayer is a member of a cooperative to which subchapter T applies, any trade or business of the cooperative described in subclause (I) shall be treated as the trade or business of the taxpayer.

children.heading: Certain losses disregarded
children.label: D Certain losses disregarded
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(i), there shall not be taken into account any deduction for any loss arising by reason of fire, storm, or other casualty, or by reason of disease or drought, involving any farming business.

children.heading: Application of subsection in case of partnerships and S corporations
children.label: 5 Application of subsection in case of partnerships and S corporations
A
children.text: this subsection shall be applied at the partner or shareholder level, and

children.label: B
children.text: each partner’s or shareholder’s proportionate share of the items of income, gain, or deduction of the partnership or S corporation for any taxable year from farming businesses attributable to the partnership or S corporation, and of any applicable subsidies received by the partnership or S corporation during the taxable year, shall be taken into account by the partner or shareholder in applying this subsection to the taxable year of such partner or shareholder with or within which the taxable year of the partnership or S corporation ends.

children.heading: Additional reporting
children.label: 6 Additional reporting
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe such additional reporting requirements as the Secretary determines appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Coordination with section 469
children.label: 7 Coordination with section 469
children.text: This subsection shall be applied before the application of section 469.

children.label: 2 Disallowed loss carryover
children.text: Any loss which is disallowed under paragraph (1) shall be treated as a net operating loss for the taxable year for purposes of determining any net operating loss carryover under section 172(b) for subsequent taxable years.

children.text: Deductions for losses from sales or exchanges of capital assets shall not be taken into account under subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Application of subsection in case of partnerships and S corporations
children.label: 4 Application of subsection in case of partnerships and S corporations
A

children.heading: Additional reporting
children.label: 5 Additional reporting
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such additional reporting requirements as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Coordination with section 469
